title: Add topologySpreadConstraints and PriorityClass support
description:
# GitHub Issue #207: Add topologySpreadConstraints and PriorityClass support to the Keystone Deployment

**Category**: improvement | **Scope**: Small

**Description**: `buildKeystoneDeployment` (operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go:150) currently produces a Deployment whose pod template has neither `topologySpreadConstraints`, nor `affinity`, nor `priorityClassName`. With the default `replicas: 3` plus the `minAvailable: 1` PDB (CC-0037, reconcile_deployment.go:284-307), nothing prevents the scheduler from coalescing all three Keystone pods onto the same node, so a single node reboot, kernel panic, or kubelet restart still produces a total Keystone outage despite the apparent 3-way HA. Add (1) sensible default `TopologySpreadConstraints` to the generated pod template — `maxSkew: 1` across `topology.kubernetes.io/zone` and `kubernetes.io/hostname`, both with `whenUnsatisfiable: ScheduleAnyway` so that the defaults never deadlock single-node dev clusters — and (2) an optional `spec.priorityClassName` field on the Keystone CR that is wired through to the pod template when set. Both should be overridable via new optional fields on the CRD: `spec.topologySpreadConstraints` and `spec.priorityClassName`.

**Motivation**: Keystone is the gating dependency for every other OpenStack service, so an unavailable Keystone takes down the entire control plane. The current setup looks HA on paper (3 replicas + PDB) but provides no anti-coalescing guarantees: a freshly-scheduled CR on a small cluster regularly lands all three pods on the same node, and even on multi-node clusters the scheduler has no signal to spread them. PriorityClass support is needed so operators can mark Keystone with a high-priority class on saturated control-plane nodes, ensuring it wins eviction races against tenant workloads. The change is intentionally small (~30 lines of generation code plus a CRD field and webhook validation) and unlocks a meaningful step up in production stability.

**Affected Areas**:
- `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go`
- `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_webhook.go`
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go`
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment_test.go`
- `operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/crds/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml`
- `operators/keystone/config/crd/bases/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml`
- `architecture/docs/04-architecture/04-high-availability.md`

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] `KeystoneSpec` gains an optional `priorityClassName string` field
- [ ] `KeystoneSpec` gains an optional `topologySpreadConstraints []corev1.TopologySpreadConstraint` field
- [ ] `buildKeystoneDeployment` sets default `TopologySpreadConstraints` of `maxSkew: 1` over `topology.kubernetes.io/zone` and `kubernetes.io/hostname`, both `whenUnsatisfiable: ScheduleAnyway`, with `LabelSelector` matching the Deployment's selector labels
- [ ] When `spec.topologySpreadConstraints` is set, it fully replaces the defaults (no merging surprises)
- [ ] When `spec.priorityClassName` is set, it is applied to the pod template; otherwise the field is left empty so admission control does not reject pods outside `kube-system`
- [ ] The validating webhook rejects a `priorityClassName` that does not exist in the cluster (lookup against `scheduling.k8s.io/v1 PriorityClass`) and rejects topology constraints whose label selector does not match the Deployment selector
- [ ] Unit tests in `reconcile_deployment_test.go` cover: defaults applied, override replaces defaults, `priorityClassName` set/unset, webhook validation paths
- [ ] CRD manifests in both `helm/.../crds/` and `config/crd/bases/` are regenerated and committed
- [ ] `architecture/docs/04-architecture/04-high-availability.md` documents the new defaults and the override fields, including a recommended `PriorityClass` definition operators should ship alongside Keystone for production

**Labels:** enhancement

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/207

stories.title: Operator deploys Keystone with default HA topology spreading
stories.want: Keystone pods to automatically spread across zones and nodes when I deploy a Keystone CR without specifying topology constraints
stories.so_that: a single node or zone failure does not take down all Keystone pods, providing genuine HA beyond the 3-replica + PDB paper guarantee
criteria: buildKeystoneDeployment produces a PodSpec with exactly 2 TopologySpreadConstraints when spec.topologySpreadConstraints is nil
First default constraint uses topologyKey=topology.kubernetes.io/zone, maxSkew=1, whenUnsatisfiable=ScheduleAnyway
Second default constraint uses topologyKey=kubernetes.io/hostname, maxSkew=1, whenUnsatisfiable=ScheduleAnyway
Both default constraints use a LabelSelector whose MatchLabels exactly equal the Deployment selector labels (app.kubernetes.io/name=keystone, app.kubernetes.io/instance=<cr-name>)

stories.title: Operator overrides topology spread constraints with custom values
stories.want: to replace the default topology spread constraints with my own set when I specify spec.topologySpreadConstraints on the Keystone CR
stories.so_that: I can adapt spreading behavior to my cluster topology (e.g., single-zone, rack-aware) without merging surprises
criteria: When spec.topologySpreadConstraints is set to a non-nil slice, the Deployment pod template uses exactly those constraints with no defaults merged in
An empty slice (spec.topologySpreadConstraints: []) produces a pod template with no TopologySpreadConstraints at all
The webhook rejects custom constraints whose LabelSelector.MatchLabels do not equal the Deployment selector labels
The webhook rejects custom constraints with a nil LabelSelector

stories.title: Operator assigns PriorityClass to Keystone pods
stories.want: to set spec.priorityClassName on the Keystone CR so that Keystone pods are assigned a PriorityClass
stories.so_that: Keystone pods win eviction races against lower-priority workloads on saturated control-plane nodes
criteria: When spec.priorityClassName is set to a non-empty string referencing an existing PriorityClass, the pod template's PriorityClassName field is set to that value
When spec.priorityClassName is empty or omitted, the pod template's PriorityClassName field is empty (no PriorityClass assigned)
The webhook rejects a priorityClassName that does not correspond to an existing scheduling.k8s.io/v1 PriorityClass in the cluster
The webhook accepts a valid priorityClassName that references an existing PriorityClass

stories.title: Operator receives clear validation errors for invalid scheduling configuration
stories.want: the validating webhook to reject invalid priorityClassName references and mismatched label selectors with clear field-path error messages
stories.so_that: I catch configuration errors at admission time before they reach the scheduler
criteria: Webhook rejection for non-existent PriorityClass includes field path spec.priorityClassName and the invalid value
Webhook rejection for mismatched TSC label selector includes field path spec.topologySpreadConstraints[i].labelSelector and describes the expected labels
Multiple validation errors are accumulated and returned together (not fail-fast on first error)
ValidateCreate and ValidateUpdate both exercise the same validation logic

stories.title: Operator reads HA documentation for topology and priority configuration
stories.want: the HA architecture documentation to describe the default topology spread behavior, the override mechanism, and a recommended PriorityClass definition
stories.so_that: I can make informed decisions about production scheduling configuration
criteria: architecture/docs/04-architecture/04-high-availability.md documents the two default TopologySpreadConstraints (zone + hostname)
Documentation explains the ScheduleAnyway policy choice (single-node dev clusters)
Documentation includes a recommended PriorityClass YAML definition for production Keystone deployments
Documentation describes how to override defaults via spec.topologySpreadConstraints

requirements.description: The system SHALL apply default TopologySpreadConstraints (maxSkew=1 over topology.kubernetes.io/zone and kubernetes.io/hostname, both ScheduleAnyway) to the Keystone Deployment pod template when spec.topologySpreadConstraints is nil
requirements.rationale: Prevents scheduler from coalescing all Keystone pods on a single node/zone, turning the 3-replica + PDB setup into genuine HA

scenarios.name: Default constraints applied when field is nil
scenarios.when: buildKeystoneDeployment is called with a Keystone CR where spec.topologySpreadConstraints is nil
scenarios.then: the Deployment pod template contains exactly 2 TopologySpreadConstraints: zone (maxSkew=1, ScheduleAnyway) and hostname (maxSkew=1, ScheduleAnyway)

scenarios.name: Default constraints use correct topology keys
scenarios.then: the first constraint uses topologyKey=topology.kubernetes.io/zone and the second uses topologyKey=kubernetes.io/hostname

requirements.description: The system SHALL fully replace default TopologySpreadConstraints with user-provided values when spec.topologySpreadConstraints is set (non-nil), with no merging of defaults
requirements.rationale: Users need predictable override behavior; merging defaults with custom constraints creates confusion about the final pod spec

scenarios.name: Custom constraints replace defaults
scenarios.when: buildKeystoneDeployment is called with a Keystone CR where spec.topologySpreadConstraints contains one custom constraint
scenarios.then: the Deployment pod template contains exactly that one constraint and no defaults

scenarios.name: Empty slice disables constraints
scenarios.when: buildKeystoneDeployment is called with a Keystone CR where spec.topologySpreadConstraints is an empty non-nil slice
scenarios.then: the Deployment pod template contains no TopologySpreadConstraints

requirements.description: The system SHALL set the pod template PriorityClassName to spec.priorityClassName when that field is non-empty
requirements.rationale: Operators need to ensure Keystone wins eviction races against tenant workloads on saturated control-plane nodes

scenarios.name: PriorityClassName applied when set
scenarios.when: buildKeystoneDeployment is called with spec.priorityClassName set to 'system-cluster-critical'
scenarios.then: the Deployment pod template PriorityClassName equals 'system-cluster-critical'

scenarios.name: PriorityClassName empty when unset
scenarios.when: buildKeystoneDeployment is called with spec.priorityClassName as empty string
scenarios.then: the Deployment pod template PriorityClassName is empty
and_then: admission control does not reject pods for referencing a non-existent PriorityClass

requirements.description: The validating webhook SHALL reject a spec.priorityClassName that does not correspond to an existing scheduling.k8s.io/v1 PriorityClass in the cluster
requirements.rationale: Catches typos and misconfigurations at admission time rather than letting pods fail to schedule

scenarios.name: Non-existent PriorityClass rejected
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is submitted with spec.priorityClassName set to 'nonexistent-class'
scenarios.then: the webhook returns a validation error with field path spec.priorityClassName
and_then: the error message indicates the PriorityClass was not found

scenarios.name: Existing PriorityClass accepted
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is submitted with spec.priorityClassName set to a PriorityClass that exists
scenarios.then: the webhook does not return a validation error for the priorityClassName field

scenarios.name: Empty PriorityClassName accepted
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is submitted with spec.priorityClassName as empty string or omitted
scenarios.then: the webhook does not perform a PriorityClass lookup and returns no error for this field

requirements.description: The validating webhook SHALL reject custom TopologySpreadConstraints whose LabelSelector does not match the Deployment's selector labels
requirements.rationale: A TSC with the wrong label selector would either spread the wrong pods or have no effect, silently breaking HA

scenarios.name: Correct label selector accepted
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR named 'my-ks' is submitted with a custom TSC whose LabelSelector.MatchLabels equals {app.kubernetes.io/name: keystone, app.kubernetes.io/instance: my-ks}
scenarios.then: the webhook does not return a validation error for the topologySpreadConstraints field

scenarios.name: Wrong label selector rejected
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is submitted with a custom TSC whose LabelSelector.MatchLabels differs from the expected deployment selector
scenarios.then: the webhook returns a validation error with field path spec.topologySpreadConstraints[i].labelSelector
and_then: the error message indicates the expected label selector values

scenarios.name: Nil label selector rejected
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is submitted with a custom TSC that has a nil LabelSelector
scenarios.then: the webhook returns a validation error with field path spec.topologySpreadConstraints[i].labelSelector
and_then: the error message indicates labelSelector is required

requirements.description: The KeystoneWebhook struct SHALL accept a client.Reader for cluster-scoped resource lookups, and validate() SHALL accept a context.Context parameter
requirements.rationale: PriorityClass validation requires reading cluster-scoped resources; context propagation is required for the API call

scenarios.name: Client injected at startup
scenarios.when: the operator starts and registers the webhook with the manager
and_then: the webhook can perform Get operations for PriorityClass resources

scenarios.name: Integration test helpers updated
scenarios.when: integration tests set up the webhook via setupEnvTest or setupEnvTestWithController
scenarios.then: the webhook receives mgr.GetClient() as its Client field

requirements.description: The system SHALL regenerate CRD manifests in both config/crd/bases/ and helm/.../crds/ after adding the new fields
requirements.rationale: CRD manifests must reflect the Go types; the Helm CRD must stay in sync per CC-0017

scenarios.name: CRD contains new fields after regeneration
scenarios.when: make manifests and make sync-crds are run in operators/keystone/
scenarios.then: both CRD YAML files contain the priorityClassName and topologySpreadConstraints properties under spec
and_then: make verify-crd-sync passes with no drift

requirements.description: The HA architecture documentation SHALL document the default TopologySpreadConstraints, the override mechanism, and a recommended PriorityClass definition
requirements.rationale: Operators need to understand the default scheduling behavior and how to customize it for production

scenarios.name: Documentation describes defaults
scenarios.then: the document describes the two default TSCs (zone + hostname, maxSkew=1, ScheduleAnyway) and explains the ScheduleAnyway policy choice

scenarios.name: Documentation includes PriorityClass recommendation
scenarios.when: an operator reads the HA documentation
scenarios.then: the document includes a recommended PriorityClass YAML definition for production Keystone deployments

tasks.title: Add priorityClassName and topologySpreadConstraints fields to KeystoneSpec, and wire them into buildKeystoneDeployment with default TSCs (REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-003)
tasks.description: In `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go`, add two new optional fields to KeystoneSpec: `PriorityClassName string` (json:"priorityClassName,omitempty", +optional) and `TopologySpreadConstraints []corev1.TopologySpreadConstraint` (json:"topologySpreadConstraints,omitempty", +optional). Place them after the Resources field following existing conventions. In `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go`, add a `defaultTopologySpreadConstraints(keystone)` helper returning the two default TSCs (zone + hostname, maxSkew=1, ScheduleAnyway, LabelSelector matching selectorLabels). In `buildKeystoneDeployment`, set `Template.Spec.TopologySpreadConstraints` from spec if non-nil, else from defaults. Set `Template.Spec.PriorityClassName` from spec.PriorityClassName.

tasks.title: Add unit tests for deployment builder: default TSCs, custom TSCs override, priorityClassName set/unset (REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-003)
tasks.description: In `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment_test.go`, add: (1) TestBuildKeystoneDeployment_DefaultTopologySpreadConstraints — verify 2 TSCs with correct topology keys, maxSkew=1, ScheduleAnyway, and correct LabelSelector; (2) TestBuildKeystoneDeployment_CustomTopologySpreadConstraintsOverrideDefaults — set spec.TopologySpreadConstraints to a single custom TSC, verify only that one is present; (3) TestBuildKeystoneDeployment_EmptyTopologySpreadConstraintsDisablesDefaults — set to empty non-nil slice, verify no TSCs; (4) TestBuildKeystoneDeployment_PriorityClassNameSet — verify pod template gets the value; (5) TestBuildKeystoneDeployment_PriorityClassNameEmpty — verify pod template PriorityClassName is empty. Follow existing test patterns using Gomega.

tasks.title: Inject client.Reader into KeystoneWebhook, add PriorityClass and TSC label selector validation (REQ-004, REQ-005, REQ-006)
tasks.description: In `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_webhook.go`: (1) Add `Client client.Reader` field to KeystoneWebhook struct. (2) Change `validate(k *Keystone)` signature to `validate(ctx context.Context, k *Keystone)`. Update ValidateCreate/ValidateUpdate to pass ctx. (3) Add PriorityClass validation: when spec.PriorityClassName is non-empty, use w.Client.Get to look up scheduling.k8s.io/v1 PriorityClass by name; if NotFound, append field.NotFound error. (4) Add TSC validation: when spec.TopologySpreadConstraints is non-nil, for each constraint validate LabelSelector is not nil and LabelSelector.MatchLabels equals the expected deployment selector labels {app.kubernetes.io/name: keystone, app.kubernetes.io/instance: k.Name}; append field.Invalid for mismatches. Add imports for schedulingv1 and sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime/pkg/client.

tasks.title: Update main.go and integration test helpers to pass client to KeystoneWebhook (REQ-006)
tasks.description: Update `operators/keystone/main.go:52` to pass mgr.GetClient() when creating KeystoneWebhook: change `(&keystonev1alpha1.KeystoneWebhook{}).SetupWebhookWithManager(mgr)` to `(&keystonev1alpha1.KeystoneWebhook{Client: mgr.GetClient()}).SetupWebhookWithManager(mgr)`. Apply the same change in `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/integration_test.go:39` and `operators/keystone/internal/controller/integration_test.go:67`.

tasks.title: Add webhook unit tests for PriorityClass validation and TSC label selector validation (REQ-004, REQ-005)
tasks.description: In `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_webhook_test.go`, add: (1) TestValidate_PriorityClassNameExistsAccepted — create fake client with a PriorityClass, set spec.priorityClassName, validate passes; (2) TestValidate_PriorityClassNameNotFoundRejected — create fake client without the PriorityClass, set spec.priorityClassName, validate returns error containing 'priorityClassName'; (3) TestValidate_PriorityClassNameEmptyAccepted — no client needed, empty priorityClassName, no error; (4) TestValidate_TopologySpreadConstraintCorrectLabelsAccepted — set spec.topologySpreadConstraints with correct MatchLabels, validate passes; (5) TestValidate_TopologySpreadConstraintWrongLabelsRejected — wrong MatchLabels, validate returns error containing 'topologySpreadConstraints'; (6) TestValidate_TopologySpreadConstraintNilSelectorRejected — nil LabelSelector, validate returns error. Tests that exercise PriorityClass lookup must create a fake client using clientgoscheme and inject it into KeystoneWebhook{Client: fakeClient}. Add schedulingv1 import.

tasks.title: Regenerate CRD manifests and sync Helm CRDs (REQ-007)
tasks.description: Run `make manifests` and `make sync-crds` in `operators/keystone/` to regenerate CRD YAML from the updated Go types. Verify the new fields (priorityClassName, topologySpreadConstraints) appear in both `config/crd/bases/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml` and `helm/keystone-operator/crds/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml`. Run `make verify-crd-sync` to confirm no drift.

tasks.title: Update HA architecture documentation with topology spread defaults and PriorityClass recommendation (REQ-008)
tasks.description: In `architecture/docs/04-architecture/04-high-availability.md`, add a new subsection under 'Control Plane HA' (before 'Data Plane HA') documenting: (1) Keystone topology spread defaults — the two default TSCs (zone + hostname, maxSkew=1, ScheduleAnyway), why ScheduleAnyway was chosen (single-node dev clusters), and the override mechanism via spec.topologySpreadConstraints; (2) A recommended PriorityClass YAML definition (e.g., value: 1000000, globalDefault: false) that operators should ship alongside Keystone for production; (3) Update the Component table to add a Keystone row with replicas=3, ScheduleAnyway topology spread + PDB, and PriorityClass support. Follow existing document structure and style.

test_specifications.story: Operator deploys Keystone with default HA topology spreading
test_specifications.expected: [unit] When spec.TopologySpreadConstraints is nil, the built Deployment should have exactly 2 TSCs: zone (maxSkew=1, ScheduleAnyway) and hostname (maxSkew=1, ScheduleAnyway), each with LabelSelector matching selectorLabels

test_specifications.story: Operator deploys Keystone with default HA topology spreading
test_specifications.expected: [unit] Default TSC LabelSelector.MatchLabels must equal {app.kubernetes.io/name: keystone, app.kubernetes.io/instance: test-keystone}

test_specifications.story: Operator overrides topology spread constraints with custom values
test_specifications.expected: [unit] When spec.TopologySpreadConstraints is set to a single custom TSC, the Deployment should contain only that TSC and no defaults

test_specifications.story: Operator overrides topology spread constraints with custom values
test_specifications.expected: [unit] When spec.TopologySpreadConstraints is an empty non-nil slice, the Deployment should have no TopologySpreadConstraints

test_specifications.story: Operator assigns PriorityClass to Keystone pods
test_specifications.expected: [unit] When spec.PriorityClassName is 'system-cluster-critical', pod template PriorityClassName equals 'system-cluster-critical'

test_specifications.story: Operator assigns PriorityClass to Keystone pods
test_specifications.expected: [unit] When spec.PriorityClassName is empty, pod template PriorityClassName is empty string

test_specifications.story: Operator receives clear validation errors for invalid scheduling configuration
test_specifications.expected: [unit] Webhook rejects spec.priorityClassName referencing a non-existent PriorityClass, error contains 'priorityClassName'

test_specifications.story: Operator assigns PriorityClass to Keystone pods
test_specifications.expected: [unit] Webhook accepts spec.priorityClassName when the referenced PriorityClass exists in the fake client

test_specifications.story: Operator assigns PriorityClass to Keystone pods
test_specifications.expected: [unit] Webhook accepts empty spec.priorityClassName without performing a PriorityClass lookup

test_specifications.story: Operator overrides topology spread constraints with custom values
test_specifications.expected: [unit] Webhook accepts custom TSC whose LabelSelector.MatchLabels matches the expected deployment selector

test_specifications.story: Operator receives clear validation errors for invalid scheduling configuration
test_specifications.expected: [unit] Webhook rejects custom TSC whose LabelSelector.MatchLabels does not match expected labels, error contains 'topologySpreadConstraints'

test_specifications.story: Operator receives clear validation errors for invalid scheduling configuration
test_specifications.expected: [unit] Webhook rejects custom TSC with nil LabelSelector, error contains 'labelSelector'

review_criteria: All 5 deployment builder tests pass: default TSCs (2 constraints with correct topology keys, maxSkew, policy, label selector), custom TSCs override defaults, empty TSCs disable defaults, priorityClassName set, priorityClassName empty
All 6 webhook validation tests pass: PriorityClass exists→accepted, not found→rejected, empty→accepted, TSC correct labels→accepted, wrong labels→rejected, nil selector→rejected
KeystoneWebhook.Client is injected in main.go and both integration test helpers (api/v1alpha1/integration_test.go and internal/controller/integration_test.go)
CRD manifests in both config/crd/bases/ and helm/.../crds/ contain priorityClassName and topologySpreadConstraints fields; make verify-crd-sync passes
No new golangci-lint warnings or go vet errors introduced by the changes
HA documentation includes: default TSC description, ScheduleAnyway rationale, override mechanism, recommended PriorityClass YAML, updated component table with Keystone row
implementation_notes:
## Architecture

```
KeystoneSpec (types.go)
├── priorityClassName: string         ← NEW
└── topologySpreadConstraints: []TSC  ← NEW
        │
        ▼
buildKeystoneDeployment (reconcile_deployment.go)
├── defaultTopologySpreadConstraints() ← NEW helper
├── PodSpec.TopologySpreadConstraints  ← wired from spec or defaults
└── PodSpec.PriorityClassName          ← wired from spec
        │
        ▼
KeystoneWebhook.validate() (keystone_webhook.go)
├── Client client.Reader               ← NEW field for PriorityClass lookup
├── validate(ctx, k)                   ← ctx added for API calls
├── PriorityClass existence check      ← NEW validation
└── TSC LabelSelector match check      ← NEW validation
```

## Data Flow

1. User submits Keystone CR with optional priorityClassName and topologySpreadConstraints
2. Mutating webhook defaults (no changes needed — no defaulting for these fields)
3. Validating webhook checks: PriorityClass exists (API lookup), TSC label selectors match deployment selector
4. Reconciler builds Deployment: if spec.topologySpreadConstraints is nil, apply defaults; otherwise use spec values. Wire priorityClassName directly.
5. Deployment applied to cluster; scheduler uses TSCs and PriorityClass for placement decisions.

## Key Decisions

- **ScheduleAnyway not DoNotSchedule**: Default TSCs use ScheduleAnyway so single-node dev clusters are not deadlocked. Production operators who need strict spreading can override.
- **Full replacement, no merging**: When spec.topologySpreadConstraints is set, defaults are fully replaced. This avoids surprises where users cannot remove a default.
- **client.Reader not client.Client**: The webhook only needs read access (Get). Using the Reader interface minimizes the surface area.
- **No nil guard on Client**: If priorityClassName is set but Client is nil (test misconfiguration), the code panics — this is intentional fail-fast behavior.
- **Label selector validation in webhook**: The webhook computes expected selector labels directly ({app.kubernetes.io/name: keystone, app.kubernetes.io/instance: k.Name}) rather than importing selectorLabels() from the controller package, maintaining package boundaries.

## Pitfalls

- All 3 webhook instantiation sites (main.go, 2 integration tests) must be updated to inject the client. Missing any one causes nil panic when PriorityClass validation runs.
- The validate() signature change (adding ctx) requires updating both ValidateCreate and ValidateUpdate method bodies.
- CRD regeneration (make manifests) must happen AFTER types.go changes are complete, or the YAML will be stale.
